```python
import pytest

def is_palindrome(s: str) -> bool:
    s = s.lower().replace(' ', '')
    return s == s[::-1]

# Happy path tests
@pytest.mark.parametrize("input_str, expected", [
    ("racecar", True),
    ("A man a plan a canal Panama", True),
    ("hello", False),
    ("Madam", True),
    ("12321", True),
    ("12345", False),
])
def test_is_palindrome_happy_path(input_str, expected):
    assert is_palindrome(input_str) == expected

# Edge cases
@pytest.mark.parametrize("input_str, expected", [
    ("", True),  # Empty string
    ("a", True),  # Single character
    (" ", True),  # Only spaces
    ("a a", True),  # Spaces between characters
    ("ab", False),  # Two different characters
    ("aa", True),  # Two same characters
    ("aA", True),  # Case insensitive
    ("  ", True),  # Multiple spaces
])
def test_is_palindrome_edge_cases(input_str, expected):
    assert is_palindrome(input_str) == expected

# Error cases
@pytest.mark.parametrize("invalid_input", [
    123,  # Integer
    [1, 2, 3],  # List
    None,  # None
    {"key": "value"},  # Dictionary
])
def test_is_palindrome_error_cases(invalid_input):
    with pytest.raises(AttributeError):
        is_palindrome(invalid_input)
```
